text:
"Ole Miss" redirects here.
University Mississippi athletics program, Miss Rebels.
confused with Mississippi State University.
university chartered Mississippi Legislature February 1844, four years later admitted first enrollment students.
university classified "R1: Doctoral University Very High Research Activity" Carnegie Classification Institutions Higher Education annual research development budget $121.6 million.
university ranked 2018 edition News Rankings Best National Universities.
Across campuses, university comprises some 23,258 students.
addition main campus Oxford medical school Jackson, university also campuses Tupelo, Booneville, Grenada, Southaven, well accredited online high school.
About percent undergraduates percent overall come from Mississippi, percent minorities; international students respectively represent different nations.
colleges universities participating National Grant Program participant National Space Grant College Fellowship Program.
Miss center activity during American civil rights movement when race riot erupted 1962 following attempted admission James Meredith, African-American, segregated campus.
While university successfully integrated that year, Confederate symbols motifs remained controversial aspect school's identity culture.
response university attempted take proactive measures rebrand image, including effectively banning display Confederate flags Vaught-Hemingway Stadium 1997, officially abandoning Colonel mascot 2003, removing "Dixie" from Pride South marching band's repertoire 2016.
2018, following racially charged rant social media alum academic building namesake, former Chancellor Jeffrey Vitter reaffirmed university's commitment "honest open dialogue about history", making campuses "more welcoming inclusive".
"Dead House" built before Civil used morgue during war.
Bodies were carried from Dead House across campus Civil Cemetery, which contains more than graves.
building demolished 1958, Farley Hall--which contains Meek School Journalism Media--was erected place.
Mississippi Legislature chartered University Mississippi February 1844.
university opened doors first class students four years later 1848.
years, university Mississippi's only public institution higher learning, years state's only comprehensive university.
Politician Pryor founding trustee.
When university opened, campus consisted buildings: dormitories, faculty houses, steward's hall, Lyceum center.
Constructed from 1846 1848, Lyceum oldest building campus.
Originally, Lyceum housed classrooms faculty offices university.
building's north south wings were added 1903, Class 1927 donated clock above eastern portico.
Lyceum home university's administration offices.
columned facade Lyceum represented official crest university, along with date establishment.
1854, university established fourth state-supported, public school United States, also began offering engineering education.
With outbreak Civil 1861, classes were interrupted when almost entire student body (135 students) from University Mississippi enlisted Confederate army, forming infantry group nicknamed University Greys.
However, students were killed during war, 100% casualty rate.
Most died during battles Gettysburg Vicksburg.
Lyceum used hospital during Civil both Union Confederate soldiers, especially those were wounded battle Shiloh.
hundred-fifty soldiers died campus hospital were buried cemetery grounds university.
During post-war period, university former Confederate general A.P.
Stewart, Rogersville, Tennessee native.
served Chancellor from 1874 1886.
university became coeducational 1882 first such institution Southeast hire female faculty member, Sarah McGehee Isom, doing 1885.
student yearbook published first time 1897.
contest held solicit suggestions yearbook title from student body.
Elma Meek, student, submitted winning entry "Ole Miss."
Meek's source term unknown; some historians theorize made diminutive "old Mississippi" derived term from "ol' missus," African-American term plantation's "old mistress."
This sobriquet only chosen yearbook, also became name which university informally known.
"Ole Miss" defined school's intangible spirit, which separate from tangible aspects university.
university began medical education 1903, when University Mississippi School Medicine established Oxford campus.
that era, university provided two-year pre-clinical education certificates, graduates went state complete doctor medicine degrees.
1950, Mississippi Legislature voted create four-year medical school.
July 1955, University Medical Center opened capital Jackson, Mississippi, four-year medical school.
University Mississippi Medical Center, called, health sciences campus University Mississippi.
houses University Mississippi School Medicine along with five other health science schools: nursing, dentistry, health-related professions, graduate studies pharmacy (The School Pharmacy split between Oxford University Mississippi Medical Center campuses).
Several attempts were made executive legislative branches Mississippi state government relocate otherwise close University Mississippi.
Mississippi Legislature between 1900 1930 introduced several bills aiming accomplish this, such legislation ever passed either house.
such bill introduced 1912 Senator William Ellis Carthage, Mississippi, which would have merged college with then-Mississippi A&M.
However, this measure soundly defeated, despite bill only seeking form exploratory committee.
February 1920, members legislature arrived campus discussed with students faculty idea consolidating A&M, College Women Miss located Jackson, rather than appropriate $750,000.00 funds requested then-Chancellor Joseph Powers which were needed repair dilapidated structurally unsound buildings campus, which discovered following partial collapse dormitory 1917 scathing review other buildings later that same year state architect.
These funds, plus additional $300,000.00 were appropriated school, which used build male dormitories, female dormitory pharmacy building, which partially resolved longstanding issue inadequate dormitory space students.
During 1930s, Mississippi Governor Theodore Bilbo, populist, tried move university Jackson.
Chancellor Alfred Hume gave state legislators grand tour Miss surrounding historic city Oxford, persuading them keep original setting.
During World colleges universities nationally that took part V-12 Navy College Training Program, which offered students path Navy commission.
Desegregation came Miss early 1960s with activities United States Force veteran James Meredith from Kosciusko, Mississippi.
Even Meredith's initial efforts required great courage.
involved knew violently William David McCain white political establishment Mississippi recently reacted similar efforts Clyde Kennard enroll Mississippi Southern College (now University Southern Mississippi).
Meredith lawsuit that allowed admission University Mississippi September 1962.
attempted enter campus September September again September only blocked Mississippi Governor Ross Barnett, proclaimed that "...No school state will integrated while your Governor.
shall everything power prevent integration schools."
After United States Court Appeals Fifth Circuit held both Barnett Lieutenant Governor Paul Johnson, contempt, with fines more than $10,000 each they refused allow Meredith enroll, Meredith, escorted force U.S.
Marshals, entered campus September 1962.
people were killed gunfire during riot, French journalist, Paul Guihard Oxford repairman, Gunter.
One-third Marshals, men, were injured, were soldiers National Guardsmen.
After control re-established federal forces, Meredith, thanks protection afforded federal marshals, able enroll attend first class October
Following riot, elements Army National Guard division were stationed Oxford prevent future similar violence.
While most Miss students riot prior official enrollment university, many harassed Meredith during first semesters campus.
According first-person accounts, students living Meredith's dorm bounced basketballs floor just above room through hours night.
When Meredith walked into cafeteria meals, students eating would turn their backs.
Meredith table with other students, whom were white, they would immediately move another table.
Many these events featured 2012 ESPN documentary film Ghosts Miss.
2002 university marked 40th anniversary integration with yearlong series events titled "Open Doors: Building Years Opportunity Higher Education."
These included oral history Miss, various symposiums, April unveiling $130,000 memorial, reunion federal marshals served campus.
September 2003, university completed year's events with international conference race.
that year, student body identified African American.
Meredith's Joseph graduated doctoral student School Business Administration.
Circle, including flagpole Confederate Monument.
Additionally, April 2010, university campus declared National Historic Site Society Professional Journalists honor reporters covered 1962 riot, including late French reporter Paul Guihard, victim riot.
From September 2012 2013, university marked 50th anniversary integration with program called Opening Closed Society, referring Mississippi: Closed Society, 1964 book James Silver, history professor university.
events included lectures figures such Attorney General Eric Holder singer activist Harry Belafonte, movie screenings, panel discussions, "walk reconciliation redemption."
Myrlie Evers-Williams, widow Medgar Evers, slain civil rights leader late president state NAACP, closed observance 2013, delivering address university's 160th commencement.
university chosen host first presidential debate 2008, between Senator John McCain then-Senator Barack Obama which held September 2008.
This first presidential debate held Mississippi.
university adopted on-field mascot athletic events fall 2010.
Colonel Reb, retired from sidelines sporting events 2003, officially replaced "Rebel", black bear, then, 2018, replaced Landshark.
university sports teams still officially referred Rebels.
university's 25th Rhodes Scholar named 2008.
Since 1998, produced Rhodes Scholars, well Goldwater Scholars, seven Truman Scholars, Fulbright Scholars, Marshall Scholar, three Udall Scholars, Gates Cambridge Scholars, Mitchell Scholar, Boren Scholars, Boren fellow German Chancellor Fellowship.
student-faculty ratio University Mississippi 19:1, school 47.4 percent classes with fewer than students.
most popular majors University Mississippi include: Integrated Marketing Communications, Elementary Education Teaching; Marketing/Marketing Management, General; Accountancy, Finance, General; Pharmacy, Pharmaceutical Sciences, Administration, Other; Biology, Psychology Criminal Justice; Business Administration Management, General.
average freshman retention rate, indicator student success satisfaction, 85.3 percent.
University Mississippi Medical Center surgeons, James Hardy, performed world's first human lung transplant, 1963, world's first animal-to-human heart transplant, 1964.
heart chimpanzee used heart transplant because Hardy's research transplantation, consisting primate studies during previous nine years.
University Mississippi Field Station Abbeville natural laboratory used study, research teach about sustainable freshwater ecosystems.
Since 1968, school operates only legal marijuana farm production facility United States.
National Institute Drug Abuse (NIDA) contracts university production cannabis approved research studies plant well distribution seven surviving medical cannabis patients grandfathered into Compassionate Investigational Drug program (established 1978 canceled 1991).
first commercial blues recording Mamie Smith's performance Perry Bradford's "Crazy Blues" 1920.
university houses largest blues music archives United States.
Some contributions collection were donated King donated entire personal record collection.
archive includes first ever commercial blues recording, song called "Crazy Blues" recorded Mamie Smith 1920.
Mamie Ellis Nassour Arts Entertainment Collection, highlighted wealth theater film scripts, photographs memorabilia, dedicated September 2005.
Center Intelligence Security Studies (CISS) delivers academic programming prepare outstanding students careers intelligence analysis both public private sectors.
addition, CISS personnel engage applied research consortium building with government, private academic partners.
late 2012, United States Director National Intelligence designated CISS Intelligence Community Center Academic Excellence (CAE).
CISS only college programs United States with this distinction.
Haley Barbour Center Manufacturing Excellence (CME) established June 2008 provide unique opportunities students interested manufacturing.
developing interdisciplinary educational opportunities within innovative academic learning model that provides students with practical experiences, fundamental knowledge, creative skill sets needed lead world modern manufacturing.
working with companies express strong desire become industry leaders wish work with staff students.
university offers Chinese Language Flagship Program (simplified Chinese: 中文旗舰项目; traditional Chinese: 中文旗艦項目; pinyin: Zhōngwén Qíjiàn Xiàngmù), study program aiming provide Americans with advanced knowledge Chinese.
Croft Institute International Studies University Mississippi privately funded, select-admissions, undergraduate program high achieving students pursue B.A.
degree international studies.
Croft students combine regional concentration Europe, East Asia, Latin America, Middle East with thematic concentration global economics business, international governance politics, social cultural identity.
program emphasizes intensive foreign language training, qualitative quantitative skills, mandatory study abroad semester more, yearlong senior thesis.
University Mississippi several student organizations help students know another adapt life university.
organization International Student Organization (ISO), which organizes activities events international students.
Notable events include cultural night, date auction international sports tournament.
Named honor distinguished Miss alumnus, former U.S. Sen. Trent Lott, Lott Leadership Institute offers wide range leadership outreach programs that work enrich lives enhance abilities students faculty, well high school students, students from other colleges universities, community.
collaborator Mississippi Excellence Teaching Program, designed attract students teacher education programs with full scholarships professional incentives.
goal attract high school seniors want become mathematics English teachers Mississippi.
Funded Robert Hearin Support Foundation, program designed create unique "honors college style" learning experience high-performing education students promote collaboration between students faculty Mississippi State University.
World Class Teaching Program special program University Mississippi School Education designed provide support teachers going through National Board Certification process.
Sally McDonnell Barksdale Honors College attracts diverse body high-performing students University Mississippi prepares citizen scholars fired life mind, committed public good driven find solutions.
Established 1997 through gift from Barksdale family, Honors College merges intellectual rigor with community action.
offers education similar that prestigious private liberal arts schools universities, lower cost.
Small, discussion-based classes, dedicated faculty nurturing staff enable honors students experience intellectual well personal growth.
University Mississippi member Academic Consortium.
renamed SECU, initiative collaborative endeavor designed promote research, scholarship achievement among member universities Southeastern Conference.
SECU formed serve means bolster collaborative academic endeavors Southeastern Conference universities.
goals include highlighting endeavors achievements faculty, students universities advancing academic reputation universities.
2013, University Mississippi participated Symposium Atlanta, Georgia which organized University Georgia Bioenergy Systems Research Institute.
topic symposium titled "Impact Southeast World's Renewable Energy Future."
University Mississippi
2012 Forbes Best Value Colleges list, part annual America's Colleges section.
only school make list.
However, failed make best value 2017.
last years, Chronicle Higher Education named University Mississippi "Great Colleges Work For", putting institution elite company.
2018 results, released Chronicle's annual report "The Academic Workplace", Miss among institutions honored from colleges universities surveyed.
2018, Miss campus ranked second safest safest nation.
U.S. News World Report ranks Professional program School Business Administration among American public universities, online program ranks
three degree programs University Mississippi's Patterson School Accountancy among 2018 annual national rankings accounting programs published journal Public Accounting Report.
undergraduate doctoral programs lead Southeastern Conference rankings, master's program second SEC.
more Miss programs have each past eight years.
Army ROTC program received eight prestigious MacArthur Awards February 2012.
Presented U.S. Army Cadet Command Gen. Douglas MacArthur Foundation, award recognizes ideals "duty, honor country" advocated MacArthur.
life-changing work Delta communities, School Pharmacy American Association Colleges Pharmacy's 2011-12 Lawrence Weaver Transformative Community Service Award.
AACP presents award annually pharmacy school that only demonstrates major commitment addressing unmet community needs through education, practice research also serves example social responsiveness others.
Miss continues premiere destination college tailgating Grove claimed second place Southern Living's "South's Best Tailgate" contest 2012.
William Winter Institute Racial Reconciliation University Mississippi honored International Association Official Human Rights Agencies with 2012 International Award.
This accolade from nonprofit organization devoted promoting civil human rights around world presented Orleans.
University Mississippi's main campus located Oxford.
There also regional campuses Booneville, DeSoto, Grenada, Tupelo.
University Mississippi Oxford original campus, beginning with only square-mile land.
main campus today contains around 1,200 acres land.
Also, University Mississippi owns golf course airport Oxford.
golf course airport considered part University Mississippi, Oxford campus well.
buildings main University Mississippi campus come from Georgian architecture; however, some newer buildings today have more contemporary architecture.
first building built Oxford campus Lyceum, only original building remaining.
construction Lyceum began 1846 completed 1848.
Lyceum served hospital soldiers Civil War.
Also campus, Croft Institute International Studies Barnard Observatory were used soldiers during civil war.
Oxford campus University Mississippi contains history with Civil War.
campus used hospital, also after soldiers died, campus served morgue.
Where Farley Hall located, prior building referred "Dead House" where bodies deceased soldiers were stored.
Architect Frank Gates designed buildings campus 1929-1930, mostly Georgian Revival architectural style, including (Old) University High School, Barr Hall, Bondurant Hall, Farley Hall (also known Lamar Hall), Faulkner Hall, Hill Hall, Howry Hall, Isom Hall, Longstreet Hall, Martindale Hall, Vardaman Hall, Cafeteria/Union Building, Wesley Knight Field House.
Today University Mississippi campus, most buildings have been completely renovated newly constructed.
There currently least residential buildings Oxford campus, with more being built.
University Mississippi campus Oxford known beauty campus.
campus been recognized multiple years, most recently, 2016, Today recognized Miss "Most Beautiful Campus".
campus grounds kept through University Mississippi's personal landscape service.
different satellite campuses that University Mississippi much smaller than main campus Oxford.
satellite campus Tupelo started running larger space 1972, DeSoto campus opened 1996, Grenada campus been operated Holmes Community College campus since 2008.
University Mississippi campus satellite campuses continue grow.
There will continue progress construction accommodate large growth student population.
Archie Manning's uniform number, official speed limit Oxford campus.
March 2012, Ross Bjork named university's athletics director.
other speed limit campus uniform number Manning (Archie's son).
There hundreds students organizations, including religious organizations.
Daily Mississippian (DM) student-published newspaper university, established 1911.
Although located Miss campus, operated largely independent newspaper students.
only college newspaper state that published five times week.
staff consists approximately editors, about writers photographers, five-person student sales staff.
Daily circulation 12,000.
award-winning publication celebrated 100th anniversary 2011-12.
TheDMonline.com online version Daily Mississippian also includes original content that supplements print publication photo galleries, videos, breaking news student blogs.
Page views average 360,000 month.
Miss student yearbook 368-page full-color book produced students.
many awards, including Gold Crown.
WUMS-FM 92.1 Rebel Radio, commercially licensed radio station.
only student-run, commercially licensed radio stations nation, with signal stretching about miles across North Mississippi.
format features alternative college rock, news talk shows.
NewsWatch student-produced, live newscast, only local newscast Lafayette County.
freshmen (students with less than credit hours) required live campus housing their first year unless they meet certain commuter guidelines.
Department Student Housing auxiliary, meaning that self-supporting does receive appropriations from state funds.
rent received from students pays housing functions such utilities, staff salaries, furniture, supplies, repairs, renovations buildings.
Most residence staff members students, including day-to-day management, conduct board members maintenance personnel.
Upon acceptance University Mississippi, housing application submitted with application fee.
cost on-campus housing ranges from approximately $4,000 more than $8,000 (the highest price being that newly renovated Village apartments) academic calendar year, depending occupancy room type.
Students (with more than credit hours) have option live campus unaffiliated housing.
Graduate students, undergraduate students aged older, students married, students with families live Village Apartments.
complex consists story buildings, adjacent University Mississippi School.
Undergraduates over married students, graduate students live one-bedroom apartments.
Graduate students students over live studio style apartments.
Students with children live two-bedroom apartments.
Children living Village Apartments zoned Oxford School District.
Residents zoned Bramlett Elementary School (PreK-1), Oxford Elementary School (2-3), Della Davidson Elementary School (4-5), Oxford Middle School (6-8), Oxford High School (9-12).
Despite relatively small number Greek-letter organizations campus, third undergraduates participate Greek life Miss.
tradition Greek life Oxford campus deep-seated one.
fact, first fraternity founded South Rainbow Fraternity, founded Miss 1848.
fraternity merged with Delta Delta 1886.
Delta Kappa Epsilon followed shortly after Miss 1850, first have house campus Mississippi.
Today, sorority chapters very large, with some boasting over active members.
Recruitment fiercely competitive potential sorority members encouraged secure personal recommendations from Miss sorority alumnae increase chances receiving invitation join eleven sororities campus.
Fraternity recruitment also fierce, with only active chapters campus.
Associated Student Body (ASB) Miss student government organization.
Students elected Senate spring semester, with leftover seats voted fall.
Senators represent groups organizations such Greek councils sports clubs, they represent their school (i.e., College Liberal Arts School Engineering).
officers also elected spring semester, following weeks campaigning.
common tradition among Miss students that, after they vote elections, they local convenience stores purchase Coca-Cola PepsiCo brand soft drinks.
Following elections, newly sworn-in officers release applications, conduct interviews, eventually choose their cabinet members coming school year.
student body, excluding Medical Center, includes 18,121 undergraduates, 2,089 graduate students, students students Doctor Pharmacy program.
African-Americans comprise 12.8 percent student body.
Nobel Prize-winning novelist William Faulkner, attended university graduate, served university's postmaster from 1921 1924.
1929, Faulkner wrote Dying University Mississippi Power House, where worked fireman night watchman.
Best-selling legal thriller writer John Grisham received Juris Doctor degree Miss Law.
United States Senators Trent Lott, Roger Wicker Thad Cochran earned their undergraduate degrees from University Mississippi, Senator Jeanne Shaheen earned master's degree.
quarterback Archie Manning attended Miss played football Rebels.
Manning's youngest son, quarterback Manning, two-time Super Bowl champion, also played Miss.
Mahesh Bhupathi, tennis player winner Grand Slam doubles titles.
